narrative:
High pressure centered in the Ohio Valley controls our weather today. Sunshine warms the dry air, leading to a mighty fine Friday. A disturbance moves east along a front stalled to our south, adding clouds tonight into Saturday. Showers will be limited to Avery County southward Saturday, mostly in the morning hours. After a pleasant Sunday, a front moves through from the north Monday, bringing widely scattered showers and thundershowers, mostly in the morning.

2027 Blue Ridge Cameo Photo Winners Announced

The second set of winning photos, the "Cameo Winners," has been announced. We receive so many amazing photos in the Photo Contest (851 this year), choosing only 15 winners is just not enough to tell the story. So, we feature an additional 36 "Cameos" throughout the calendar. See our Photo Contest page to see this second set of winning photos.

Fine Friday

Last Updated Fri May 29 at 3:55PM by Wayne Presnell

High pressure centered in the Ohio Valley controls our weather today. Sunshine warms the dry air, leading to a mighty fine Friday. A disturbance moves east along a front stalled to our south, adding clouds tonight into Saturday. Showers will be limited to Avery County southward Saturday, mostly in the morning hours. After a pleasant Sunday, a front moves through from the north Monday, bringing widely scattered showers and thundershowers, mostly in the morning.

2027 Blue Ridge Cameo Photo Winners Announced

The second set of winning photos, the "Cameo Winners," has been announced. We receive so many amazing photos in the Photo Contest (851 this year), choosing only 15 winners is just not enough to tell the story. So, we feature an additional 36 "Cameos" throughout the calendar. See our Photo Contest page to see this second set of winning photos.

Forecast Discussion

The larger pattern went through a major shift Wednesday and Thursday. A trough is digging into the Northeast and eventually most of the Eastern U.S. A surface front moved south through the Southern Appalachians Thursday and is now stalled to our south.   Today is near-perfect, under the control of high pressure centered to our northwest. Enjoy!   A disturbance moves east along the stalled front to our south Saturday. Clouds increase tonight, and a stray shower is not impossible along the NC/TN border late tonight south of Wolf Laurel. Saturday, a few showers develop from Avery County southward, mostly limited to the morning. Temperatures are less warm Saturday because of the clouds.   We go back to pleasant weather Sunday with high pressure centered over Virginia.   A secondary front moves through our region Monday from the north. Showers are possible in Virginia and along the NC/VA border by sunrise. Then widely scattered showers and thundershowers will be in the region Monday, mostly in the morning.   Tuesday and Wednesday, high pressure in the Great Lakes drives Monday's front far to our south, and we are set for pleasant days and cool nights for the rest of the workweek. Temperatures gradually warm after Wednesday. It's awesome weather, y'all!

Detailed forecast discussion and analysis is only available to RaysWeather supporters.

Latest Insights
Monday Message for May 25, 2026

It's the Memorial Day Monday Message, where we recap our sorely-needed drought relief and discuss our region's cyclical warm/cool periods.

May 25, 2026
Rainy, Cool Saturday

It's a rainy, cool Saturday around western North Carolina and Southwest Virginia. High temperatures are running as much as 20-degrees below normal in the heart of the cold air damming zone.

Sunday and Memorial Day will continue to be unsettled, but temperatures will moderate and there will be additional breaks in the clouds, as the rainfall pattern becomes more convective, or showery in nature.

While the rainfall is falling on our holiday weekend, it's entirely beneficial with respect to our long-standing drought conditions.
